VI Business is fully compliant with all Department of Telecommunications (DoT) unified licensing and TRAI QoS norms.

Liability is capped at 100% of the total fees paid by the Customer for the specific affected Service during the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the claim event.
Liability is capped at 100% of the total annualized contract value (Annual TCV) for the specific year in which the claim arises.
VI Business cannot accept unlimited liability or liability extending to indirect, punitive, or consequential damages (e.g., loss of profits/revenue).
Termination for convenience is not permitted during the initial lock-in period (typically 36 months).
Termination for convenience is permitted after 18 months, subject to a 90-day notice period and mandatory full repayment of unrecovered capital/installation costs.
Termination must not allow the customer to walk away without payment of stranded hardware values.
VI Business reserves the unilateral right to intercept, monitor, and hand over data to Indian governmental authorities as mandated by the Unified License, without prior notice to the customer.
Lawful interception compliance is a regulatory mandate with no fallback wording permitted.
If a customer RFP requests 'Unlimited Liability,' it must be marked 'Partially Compliant' or 'Non-Compliant,' with the 'Fallback Wording' from Section 3.1 included in the deviation column.
Compliance matrices must never mark 'Compliant' on red-line terms for 'Unlimited Liability' customer RFPs.

The solution must ensure all transaction data and logs comply with the DPDP Act 2023 by siloing ingress/egress logs in Indian SOC facilities.

All exposed VI Business integration endpoints are secured exclusively via TLS 1.3.
Strict token-based authentication is mandated for all integrations.
Permitted authentication flows include OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Grant for server-to-server e-Bonding.
Permitted authentication flows include Mutual TLS (mTLS) certificate pinning for high-security banking/financial connections.
Static API keys are explicitly deprecated for Tier-1 integrations.
Support SLAs defined in this handbook are contractually legally binding metrics. Modifications require formal legal review before RFP submission.
The Enterprise CISO oversees dedicated enterprise SOC operations and governs data privacy/DPDP Act compliance independently of network engineering variables.
Threat Intelligence feeds are integrated into DPDP Act-compliant Indian-hosted SIEM instances (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel or IBM QRadar).
The VI Business (India) support SLA framework explicitly excludes unauthorized or gray-market hardware deployments.
